agent_notes: Hillingdon is a sparse PAINTS district: strongest fits are heritage pubs, Ruislip/Manor Farm, the new Hillingdon Museum, and Ruislip Woods/Ruislip Lido.

No Books & Coffee venues were included because live searches found libraries, author events, and chain book retail, but not an independent bookshop-cafe or coffee-led venue with substantive books that met PAINTS v6.
Creative Walks are limited to Ruislip Woods / Ruislip Lido, which is on the PAINTS safety-first shortlist; non-approved Hillingdon parks were rejected for walk sourcing.
Several venues are operationally promising but require host confirmation for 8-12 walk-in tolerance before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Standard hours not fully surfaced on official page; venue is current and active. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing event.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookable pub; walk-ins likely for drinks but group of 8-12 must be confirmed by host.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely if arriving off-peak or calling ahead; historic rooms may split group at peak times. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic multi-room pub/restaurant; exact unbooked seating capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 18:00 or Sunday 16:00, avoiding Friday/Saturday dinner crush.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper Hillingdon story venue: a 16th-century building tied to the 1645 Treaty of Uxbridge, with enough atmosphere to support a show-and-tell prompt about compromise, civil war, and old Middlesex. It has far more local texture than a generic high-street bar. It also gives PAINTS a strong outer-west counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: Former Place House, later The Crown and Treaty, hosted negotiations between Charles I and Parliament in 1645; sources also note Grade II* heritage and Sir John Soane-associated alterations.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local ales, pub food, historic rooms and panelling.
venues.safety_notes: Central Uxbridge, short walk to tube/bus interchange; evening dispersal is urban and well-lit but host should walk the station route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk via St John's Road; verify live routing on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Group walk-in and current bank-holiday hours require direct call; obviousness penalty because it is the headline historic pub in Uxbridge.

venues.walk_in_policy: Pub with booking option; walk-ins appear normal, but group 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Family-run canal pub; likely workable off-peak, call ahead for a single table.
venues.capacity_notes: Food and bar service; exact unreserved capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 18:00; avoid karaoke/live-event peaks if listed.
venues.why_fits_paints: Less famous than the Crown & Treaty, but warmer as a conversational pub and useful as a local canal-side anchor. Its family-run positioning and vegan menu make it more inclusive than the standard suburban pub. Tier B because the story and cultural validation are lighter.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA lists it as a Grade II listed canal pub; the official site positions it as a local, family-run Uxbridge pub.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub food, beer, vegan options, canal-pub atmosphere.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from Uxbridge core; host should guide attendees along lit main-road route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk; verify live routing on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not enough arts/craft programming for Tier A; check event nights and walk-in seating by phone.

venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub/craft bar; likely walk-in by nature, but group of 8-12 seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a standing/seated drinks circle if off-peak; may be too compact at peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Micropub scale; no published group capacity.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small craft beer and wine room on Ruislip High Street gives PAINTS a genuinely local, non-chain drinks option. The beer-guide validation and narrow format make it a better show-and-tell setting than most suburban pubs. It is strongest as an early, mellow drinks event rather than a busy Friday night.
venues.anecdote_story: London Beer Guide lists it as a micropub and new entry in 2019, with gravity cask, keg taps, and bottles/cans.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, real ale, wine, cider.
venues.safety_notes: Ruislip High Street and station area are practical for arrivals; keep start/end before late-night dispersal.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-room capacity is the main risk; call ahead even if the event model is walk-in.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Mon-Fri 12:00-23:00, Sat 11:30-23:00, Sun 11:30-22:00; kitchen hours also published.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookable pub/brasserie; walk-ins likely but 8-12 group tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better physical capacity than a micropub; likely needs a heads-up call.
venues.capacity_notes: Pub section plus brasserie; private dining exists but would require booking and changes the PAINTS model.
venues.why_fits_paints: A cosier Grade II listed pub alternative in Ruislip with stronger capacity than Hop & Vine. It is polished enough for groups but less off-grid, so Tier B. Useful as an operational backup with visible heritage.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA notes the pub originally opened in 1874 and is Grade II listed.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub/brasserie food, beer, wine, historic pub setting.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Ruislip station and main roads; straightforward after-dark routing.
venues.risks_watchouts: Heartwood/White Brasserie polish risks feeling less independent; group walk-in still needs confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official page describes current pub offer; exact standard hours not reliably extracted. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Traditional pub; group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable outside peak dining, especially garden/early evening; confirm single-area seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Country-pub layout with dining and drinking spaces; no published walk-in group capacity.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of the borough's most PAINTS-coded pub settings: a village-edge country pub rather than a retail-centre bar. It gives a good prompt around old Eastcote, village survival, and suburban London that still feels rural. Stronger as an atmosphere venue than as a food-led booking.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as a traditional country pub in Eastcote Village serving craft beer, ale, and a seasonal British menu.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, ale, seasonal pub food, village pub interiors.
venues.safety_notes: Eastcote Village is quieter than Uxbridge; host should set a clear station route or encourage paired departures after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours need direct verification; lack of strong press keeps score below top tier despite aesthetic fit.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Mon-Thu 13:00-22:30, Fri-Sat 12:00-00:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; Instagram has previously flagged temporary hours, so host must reconfirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub; walk-ins likely but group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable off-peak; live music/sports programming may make conversation difficult.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional pub with lounge/restaurant/garden; exact capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: A family-run Eastcote pub with a countryside feel and local music texture. It is useful as a more everyday, less polished counterpoint to The Case Is Altered. Tier B because conversation quality may suffer on sports/live-music nights.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue describes itself as family run, maintaining traditional countryside ambience; CAMRA records it as an Asset of Community Value.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale, pub food, live music, sports, garden.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet village setting; host should manage station walk after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Temporary hours and programming need checking; avoid Friday live band slots for PAINTS conversations.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Harefield, nearest rail/tube by bus from Uxbridge
venues.opening_hours: Current Brunning & Price site active; exact hours not reliably extracted from official contact page. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations available; walk-ins may be possible but a group of 8-12 requires confirmation.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Physical capacity strong, including private dining for up to 22 by prior arrangement; PAINTS walk-in model is weaker here.
venues.capacity_notes: Large pub with terrace/garden and private dining room; remote location makes backup fragile.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday lunch-to-afternoon in daylight; avoid dark winter arrivals by public transport.
venues.why_fits_paints: The view over the Colne Valley is unusually cinematic for London and gives attendees something immediate to talk about. It is a strong aesthetic fit but operationally fragile because of its remote Harefield location and lack of a qualifying same-category backup within 10 minutes. Tier B despite high beauty.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub's own history page calls it comparatively young, around 80 years old; CAMRA notes it was built as a country house, later became a B&B/restaurant, and opened as The Old Orchard in 2010.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ales, wine, pub food, terrace views over the Colne Valley.
venues.safety_notes: Daylight recommended; attendees should not be left to navigate rural lanes alone after dark.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest plausible same-category alternatives are Harefield/Cowley pubs beyond PAINTS radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight backup, car/bus dependence, and walk-in group policy all need host control.

venues.opening_hours: SquareMeal lists Mon-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-01:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; official site active with menus/offers.
venues.walk_in_policy: Restaurant/bar with booking; walk-in group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely physically suitable, but function/wedding venue use can disrupt casual availability.
venues.capacity_notes: Family-run restaurant/bar/function venue with canal-side outdoor space; exact walk-in capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 17:00 or Sunday 16:00, avoiding Saturday DJ/wedding periods.
venues.why_fits_paints: Canal-side setting gives the venue a visual hook and a softer Cowley option outside Uxbridge town centre. It is not as culturally sharp as the historic pubs, but the waterside texture helps. Tier B because the backup radius is weak and private-event risk is real.
venues.anecdote_story: OpenTable describes it as a family-run independent restaurant, bar, function and wedding venue on the Grand Union Canal.
venues.safety_notes: Use daylight or early evening; towpath/canal routes should not be the default route after dark for solo attendees.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - Malt Shovel and Swan & Bottle are plausible but outside the strict walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Private hire, DJ/event programming, and no tight drinks backup make this operationally fragile.

venues.venue_name: Duck Pond Market at Ruislip Manor Farm
venues.opening_hours: Duck Pond lists Ruislip Artisan & Food Market on 1st and 3rd Sundays, 10:00-15:00; July 2026 event listed for Sunday 19 July 2026, 10:00-15:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no individual booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for 8-12 as a dispersed browse-and-regroup event.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor/heritage-site market with food and craft stalls; weather resilience partly supported by the Great Barn setting but still seasonal.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the cleanest Hillingdon Market Days fit: independent makers and food producers inside the Manor Farm heritage site. It has the right gentle local energy and a named cultural anchor, not just stalls in a car park. Tier B only because there is no qualifying market backup within the 15-minute PAINTS radius.
venues.anecdote_story: Duck Pond says it launched in 2008 and describes Ruislip Manor Farm as a special ancient rural green space; event pages say the market has been at Ruislip/Manor Farm for 15+ years.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent food producers, crafts, homewares, arts, street food; heritage buildings at Manor Farm.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; Manor Farm/Ruislip town centre arrival routes are practical.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup market found within 15 min walk/transit inside Hillingdon; use Manor Farm House / Cow Byre as cultural wet-weather fallback only, not same-category market backup.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category market backup within 15 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather, stall mix varies, and no same-category backup; confirm event date and market type before publishing.

venues.venue_name: Ruislip Lido and Ruislip Woods Loop
venues.neighbourhood_station: Ruislip Lido, bus links from Ruislip/Northwood Hills
venues.opening_hours: Open public park/woodland access; Water's Edge meeting/backup opens 09:00-21:00 per Stonehouse listing. Host must confirm any seasonal car park or path restrictions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access walk; no booking required. Meeting/backup venue walk-in group tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 on a daylight hosted walk; includes uneven paths, stiles and slopes on council led-walk route.
venues.capacity_notes: Route should be hosted as one group with 3-4 stops: Lido beach/railway, Woodland Centre, ancient oak/hornbeam coppice, Poor's Field/lake edge.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's strongest Creative Walk: a PAINTS-approved safety-first site with ancient woodland, lake, miniature railway lore, and a natural regroup point at the cafe/pub. It lets people talk while moving through varied textures rather than just looping a generic park. The council already runs a led walk here, which improves operational confidence.
venues.anecdote_story: Hillingdon describes Ruislip Woods as Greater London's largest block of ancient semi-natural woodland; Ruislip Woods Trust says parts are remnants of wildwood after the last ice age.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ancient woodland, lido beach, woodland centre, miniature railway, lake edge, wildlife.
venues.safety_notes: Ruislip Woods / Ruislip Lido is on PAINTS safety-first shortlist. Daylight only; avoid isolated woodland sections near dusk and wet/icy path conditions.
venues.backup_walk_time: 0-5 min from start point, depending on exact meeting point.
venues.risks_watchouts: Water's Edge has weak Tripadvisor sentiment, so use only as weather shelter/operational fallback; car parks can fill in hot weather.

venues.venue_name: Ruislip Woods Red Route to the Lido
venues.neighbourhood_station: Wiltshire Lane / Ruislip Lido, H13 bus
venues.opening_hours: Open public route; council describes the red route as 1.2 km starting at Wiltshire Lane and ending at the Lido car park. Host must confirm daylight conditions and path closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access walk; no booking required. Weather backup walk-in group tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a shorter, slower creative prompt walk; use a host and avoid dusk.
venues.capacity_notes: 3-4 stops: Wiltshire Lane entrance, trail markers, coppice woodland, Lido car park/shore finish.
venues.why_fits_paints: A shorter route gives PAINTS a lower-commitment version of the Ruislip Woods idea. It is less varied than the full Lido loop, but better for a mixed mobility group if hosted carefully. Tier B due to smaller story arc and weaker weather backup quality.
venues.anecdote_story: Council route notes use waymarked posts; the wider reserve is a National Nature Reserve and Site of Special Scientific Interest.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Waymarked woodland route, ancient oak/hornbeam coppice, Lido finish.
venues.safety_notes: Ruislip Woods is on PAINTS safety-first shortlist. Daylight only; host should keep the group together because the start and finish differ.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min at finish; start-point backup weaker, so host should meet at Lido if rain is likely.
venues.risks_watchouts: Different start/finish complicates rainy-day backup; use Lido start if weather is uncertain.

venues.opening_hours: Council lists Museum Mon-Sat 10:00-16:00, Sun 10:00-13:00; opened 17 Dec 2025.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum; walk-in entry appears accepted. Group of 8-12 should be confirmed with museum before publishing.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted small-group museum day if the group does not block archive/museum spaces.
venues.capacity_notes: Located alongside Uxbridge Library in the Civic Centre; exact gallery capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: The newly opened museum gives Hillingdon a current, borough-specific cultural anchor rather than sending people into central London. The exhibitions are highly local, which makes them good for matched strangers sharing place-memory prompts. It is the strongest museum/gallery pick in the district.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in December 2025 with Hillingdon's Treasures and Shopping in Hillingdon, including rare artefacts and archives from the borough's past.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hillingdon's Treasures, Shopping in Hillingdon, archives, maps, photographs, prints, paintings, objects and local-history displays.
venues.safety_notes: Civic Centre/Uxbridge town-centre arrival is straightforward; use daylight/early evening museum hours.
venues.backup_venue: The Crown & Treaty as cultural-historic drinks fallback; no true museum/gallery backup within 10 min that clears current-exhibition criteria.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min walk, but category mismatch; primary remains Tier A because Hillingdon Museum is operationally robust and free.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current-exhibition press is council-led rather than independent arts press; group capacity needs confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists visitor centre open seven days per week 10:00-16:30, last admission 15:30.
venues.walk_in_policy: Visitor centre/exhibition can be visited around tour slots; guided bunker tours require timed booking. Group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if hosted around exhibition plus booked/confirmed tour; timed tour model caps spontaneity.
venues.capacity_notes: 2,000 sq m visitor centre, 95-seat auditorium, cafe, shop, toilets; tours leave promptly.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:00-12:30, especially for Secrets Uncovered dates if booked.
venues.why_fits_paints: The bunker has one of the borough's strongest stories: the No. 11 Group Operations Room behind the Battle of Britain. The visitor centre is operationally strong, with cafe and exhibition infrastructure. Tier B because the timed-tour element is not naturally walk-in and the category fit is heritage/military rather than visual arts-led.
venues.anecdote_story: The official site presents the underground operations-room story and runs Secrets Uncovered tours of hidden rooms, corridors and objects.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Museum displays, operations-room history, hidden rooms/tours, cafe, shop.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime museum visit recommended; not an evening venue. Host should manage transport from Uxbridge station.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 25 min walk / short taxi-bus hop; outside PAINTS 10-min walking rule.
venues.risks_watchouts: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min and timed-tour requirements cap it at Tier B.

venues.opening_hours: Council page lists opening hours 09:30-15:30; venue-hire page says exhibitions run Sunday to Saturday with daytime access options.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery appears open-access during exhibitions; current show and group walk-in tolerance are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small gallery; 8-12 suitable if hosted quietly and not during private view install/deinstall.
venues.capacity_notes: Council culture brochure lists Cow Byre Gallery maximum capacity around 40.
venues.best_time_slot: Market Sunday 11:00-13:00, pairing with Manor Farm/Duck Pond if exhibition is live.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small art room inside the Manor Farm heritage site gives Hillingdon an intimate gallery option rather than a big institution. It works best when paired with the Great Barn and market day. Tier B because current exhibition programming is variable and not consistently press-covered.
venues.anecdote_story: Council describes Cow Byre as an art exhibition space on the Manor Farm site, giving artists the opportunity to showcase and sell work in a popular heritage setting.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local art exhibitions, rotating artist/group shows, Manor Farm heritage surroundings.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; safe as part of Manor Farm/Ruislip High Street circuit.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk on the Manor Farm site.
venues.risks_watchouts: Must verify current exhibition before scheduling; otherwise it drops below PAINTS category threshold.

venues.opening_hours: Council says due to volunteer shortage Manor Farm House can only be opened during week/weekends upon request by emailing artsvenues@hillingdon.gov.uk.
venues.walk_in_policy: Not reliable walk-in; opening is by request. Capped at Tier B and must be host-booked/confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if opened on request; not suitable as unconfirmed walk-in event.
venues.capacity_notes: Visitor centre in Tudor Manor Farm House; hire page lists Manor Farm House maximum capacity 50.
venues.best_time_slot: Use only as a pre-arranged Saturday/Sunday daytime heritage session, ideally paired with Duck Pond Market.
venues.why_fits_paints: The house has deep local texture: Tudor building, medieval Manor Farm history, old wallpaper, artefacts, and audio-guide potential. It is exactly the sort of off-grid heritage space PAINTS likes. Operationally, though, request-only opening makes it a backup/host-arranged pick rather than a robust public walk-in.
venues.anecdote_story: Council says the Tudor house includes one of the oldest examples of domestic wallpaper still on its original wall in England, plus permanent display rooms and farming tools in the Pram Shed.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent display rooms, artefacts, interactive displays, video interpretation, temporary exhibitions in the Provost Parlour, farming tools.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Manor Farm/Ruislip setting; station route manageable.
venues.risks_watchouts: Request-only opening is the binding constraint; do not publish without written confirmation.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Chain bookshop inside a shopping centre/lower mall context; no independent cafe-bookshop identity and fails PAINTS Books & Coffee requirement.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Manor Farm Library / library author events
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Strong book and author-programming signal, including Coffee & Crime, but it is a council library/event format rather than an independent bookshop-cafe or coffee venue with substantive books for a 2-hour stay.
Search result is Uxbridge, Ontario, Canada, not Uxbridge in the London Borough of Hillingdon.
Could not verify a current curated producer/maker market in Hayes that meets PAINTS standards; historic/social references are not enough.
Hillingdon council lists a Grand Union led walk, but PAINTS v6 permits only specified canal stretches; Grand Union beyond Little Venice is explicitly not permitted for Creative Walks.
Potentially attractive heritage garden, but it is not on the PAINTS approved Creative Walk safety list; not sourced as a walk.
Hotel bar setting and CAMRA notes indicate hotel/resident-bar issues/long-term closure uncertainty; PAINTS says no hotel bars unless exceptional and independently robust.
Attractive listed canal-side pub, but Vintage Inns chain feel and weak 10-minute same-category backup pairing make it less PAINTS-coded than selected Uxbridge/Cowley options.
Historic/riverside pub, but Chef & Brewer chain operation and less distinctive PAINTS story than Crown & Treaty or General Eliott.
Permanently closed in 2018; not a current venue.
